Main Street Capital Access Atlas

Launch focus

Illinois county spotlight

Illinois is the first curated state view, showing major population centers, university communities, regional anchors, suburban counties, and smaller counties in one public-data frame.

Use these examples as starting points for local verification, not as a ranking of places or a substitute for local knowledge.

Featured briefs

County examples by local context

These groupings make the launch focus easier to scan while keeping data quality, business landscape, and branch context visible.

Major metro

Large population center with many overlapping public-data signals.

Cook, IL Signal 64.3 | Quality B 134,513 establishments 1,139 branches | 2.2 per 10k residents 13.3% poverty signal

Suburban counties

Large suburban economies where county averages may hide local variation.

Lake, IL Signal 61.5 | Quality B 20,076 establishments 152 branches | 2.1 per 10k residents 8.1% poverty signal DuPage, IL Signal 57.3 | Quality B 34,121 establishments 252 branches | 2.7 per 10k residents 6.4% poverty signal

Regional centers

University, government, industrial, and regional-service counties for comparison.

Winnebago, IL Signal 63.5 | Quality B 6,286 establishments 63 branches | 2.2 per 10k residents 16.0% poverty signal Peoria, IL Signal 62.0 | Quality B 4,168 establishments 48 branches | 2.7 per 10k residents 15.4% poverty signal St. Clair, IL Signal 55.6 | Quality B 4,973 establishments 68 branches | 2.7 per 10k residents 13.6% poverty signal Champaign, IL Signal 54.3 | Quality B 4,237 establishments 71 branches | 3.4 per 10k residents 18.0% poverty signal Sangamon, IL Signal 50.3 | Quality B 4,925 establishments 76 branches | 3.9 per 10k residents 12.9% poverty signal McLean, IL Signal 48.0 | Quality B 3,481 establishments 48 branches | 2.8 per 10k residents 13.0% poverty signal

Rural and smaller counties

Smaller county examples where source coverage and local context are especially important.

Adams, IL Signal 49.3 | Quality B 1,787 establishments 32 branches | 5.0 per 10k residents 12.3% poverty signal McDonough, IL Signal 47.3 | Quality B 611 establishments 17 branches | 6.6 per 10k residents 19.9% poverty signal Washington, IL Signal 26.6 | Quality B 364 establishments 13 branches | 9.6 per 10k residents 7.8% poverty signal

Interpretation note: county-level public data can hide neighborhood-level variation. Use this table to identify questions, then verify with local partners.

Main Street Capital Access Atlas

Public data to start better conversations about small-business support. Built independently; no private data.

This project is built independently using public data. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by any financial institution, government agency, or data publisher.
